skill.name: commit-message
skill.description: Format git commit messages combining Conventional Commits summary lines with Linux kernel-style bodies. Use when writing, reviewing, or formatting commit messages.
skill.summary: Create structured Git commit messages with Conventional Commit headers and clear explanatory bodies.
skill.author: Chemiseblanc

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found only Markdown examples, Conventional Commit placeholders, a sample commit SHA, and example security wording; no executable command, cryptographic operation, system probing, network access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: False positive: Markdown examples flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are fenced Markdown examples and inline formatting guidance for commit messages. They do not instruct the agent to execute shell, Ruby, or system commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed content is Markdown guidance and sample commit messages. No backtick command substitution, Ruby code, or executable command syntax is present in the cited ranges.

low_findings.title: False positive: Weak cryptography pattern not present
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain a skill description, a placeholder summary format, character limit guidance, and body-writing guidance. They do not define or recommend weak cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic API, hash selection, encryption routine, or security-sensitive implementation instruction. The static labels do not match the semantic content of these lines.

low_findings.title: False positive: System reconnaissance wording is example text
low_findings.description: The flagged line appears inside an example breaking-change commit message. It describes client behavior and does not request host, process, environment, or network reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is part of a commit-message example, not an operational instruction. No evidence of system probing commands or data collection behavior was found.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose context when commit messages are too vague or inconsistent. This skill formats commits with clear Conventional Commit headers and useful bodies.
actual_capabilities: Formats summary lines with type, optional scope, and imperative descriptions.
Creates commit bodies that explain motivation, impact, and context.
Handles breaking-change syntax with an exclamation marker before the colon.
Removes trailer lines such as Signed-off-by, Reviewed-by, and Fixes.
limitations: Does not inspect Git history unless the user provides relevant diff or context.
Does not validate project-specific commit policies beyond the documented format.
Does not generate trailers because the skill explicitly omits them.
Does not replace human review for sensitive release or compliance wording.

use_cases.title: Prepare commits during feature work
use_cases.description: Turn a feature summary and implementation notes into a concise commit message before committing changes.

use_cases.title: Improve review history
use_cases.description: Rewrite unclear local commit messages so reviewers can understand the reason and user impact.

use_cases.title: Standardize team workflows
use_cases.description: Apply a shared commit style across repositories that use Conventional Commits and explanatory bodies.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Commit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a small change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a commit message for this change: [describe the change]. Use the commit-message skill format.

prompt_templates.title: Format From a Diff Summary
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt when the user has a short diff summary.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a commit message from this diff summary: [paste summary]. Include a body that explains why the change was needed.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite an Existing Message
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for editing a rough commit message.
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this commit message using the skill rules. Keep the meaning, improve the summary, and remove any trailers: [paste message].

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Breaking Change Commit
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for API or behavior changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a breaking-change commit message for this change: [describe behavior change]. Explain migration impact and use the correct summary syntax.

output_examples.input: Parser fix: empty input currently crashes.
output_examples.output: fix(parser): handle empty input without panic

output_examples.input: Auth feature: add PKCE support because mobile clients cannot store secrets.
output_examples.output:
feat(auth): add OAuth2 PKCE flow support

Mobile and SPA clients cannot securely store client secrets. PKCE allows these clients to authenticate without exposing credentials in client-side code.

output_examples.input: API change: all endpoints now require authentication.
output_examples.output:
feat(api)!: require authentication for all endpoints

Anonymous access complicated rate limiting and weakened the security model. Requiring authentication enables per-user quotas and consistent access control.

best_practices: Provide the changed files, user impact, and reason for the change.
Keep the summary short, imperative, and specific to one change.
Use the body to explain context that the diff alone cannot show.
anti_patterns: Do not paste secrets, private tokens, or sensitive incident details into prompts.
Do not combine unrelated changes into one broad commit message.
Do not add trailers when using this skill because its rules omit them.

faq.question: Does this skill follow Conventional Commits?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses the type, optional scope, and description structure from Conventional Commits.

faq.question: Can it write longer commit bodies?
faq.answer: Yes. It can add a body that explains why the change matters and what impact users should expect.

faq.question: Does it include Signed-off-by or Fixes trailers?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly omits all trailers.

faq.question: Can it handle breaking changes?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses an exclamation marker before the colon and explains migration impact in the body.

faq.question: Does it read my repository automatically?
faq.answer: No. Provide the diff, summary, or context you want reflected in the message.

faq.question: Is this useful with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
